response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with a strong craftsmanship background, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no indication of Japanese proficiency (a native-level requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no embedded networking or security-feature work (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching), and his C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project. The profile is a fundamental mismatch for an embedded LAN-switch security position.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some C/C++ and Linux exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, Linux audio thesis project)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a hard native-level requirement
No embedded networking, switch, or security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF, kernel work)
C programming experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; career is centered on web/full-stack development

key_factors.factor: No embedded network-security or Linux kernel experience
